lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm looking for the blast furnace?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Do I look like a guide to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you look like a hard-working dwarf, but can you please tell me where the blast furnace is?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Alright, just head down the stairs, it's easy to find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Don't I look okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you were any shorter you wouldn't exist.

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Very funny, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you dwarves doing in this factory?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Working of course, can't you see that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But working on what?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Refining the ore that is being brought into the factory, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what does that mean?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: It means you should stop asking so many questions and get back to work!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who owns this factory?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: The Consortium does and that's all you need to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what company? I thought there were all these different companies?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Oh yes, all the major companies own this plant. It's too vital to be in the hands of one company alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what exactly are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: I tire of these questions. Let me get back to work!
